  Curriculum
  10 Weeks of Real Python
  Every week builds directly on the last — from variables to deployed ML models. No random topic jumps.

        03

      Curriculum

        01C FundamentalsVariables, data types, operators, control flow, and the compilation pipeline from .c to executable.gcc / clangint/float/charif/for/whilescanf/printf
        02Functions & ScopePrototypes, pass-by-value, recursion, and understanding the call stack frame by frame.PrototypesRecursionStack framesReturn values
        03Arrays & StringsArrays as contiguous memory, pointer-array duality, C-strings, and standard string library functions.ArraysC-stringsstring.hPointer arithmetic
        04Pointers & MemoryThe heart of C. Pointer types, dereferencing, pointer-to-pointer, and visualising memory layouts.* and &Pointer typesvoid*NULL

        06Structs & UnionsCustom data types, struct padding, typedef, and building simple linked data structures from scratch.structuniontypedefPadding
        07File I/Ofopen, fread, fwrite, fprintf — reading and writing binary and text files, and error handling with errno.fopen/fcloseBinary fileserrnofseek
        08Data Structures in CImplement linked lists, stacks, queues, and a basic hash table using only C — no libraries, no shortcuts.Linked ListStackQueueHash Table
      
    

    
      
        Memory Model
        You'll actually understand this.
        Most courses skip over memory. This one makes it the centrepiece — because everything else flows from it.
        
          Stack — grows downward
          0x7fff...f0main()← current frame
          0x7fff...d0int x = 42local variable
          0x7fff...c8int *p← pointer on stack
          Heap — grows upward
          0x55...1000malloc(100)← *p points here
          0x55...1064[free block]must call free()
        
      
    

    
      Projects
      Four Things You'll Build in C
      No toy programs. Each project solves a real problem and teaches you something you can't learn from reading.

  Track 01 · Systems Programming
  C:Closestto the machine.
  The language that built Unix, Linux, and almost everything underneath modern software. Learn C and you'll understand how computers actually work — memory, pointers, and all.
  
    ⏱ Duration 8 Weeks
    👥 Level Beginner
    📦 Projects 4 Builds
    🔑 Prereq Git & GitHub
  
  
    Enroll Now →
    View Curriculum
  



  Curriculum
  8 Weeks, Zero Hand-Holding
  C doesn't hide things from you — and neither does this course. You'll leave knowing exactly what happens when your code runs.
  
    01C FundamentalsVariables, data types, operators, control flow, and the compilation pipeline from .c to executable.gcc / clangint/float/charif/for/whilescanf/printf
    02Functions & ScopePrototypes, pass-by-value, recursion, and understanding the call stack frame by frame.PrototypesRecursionStack framesReturn values
    03Arrays & StringsArrays as contiguous memory, pointer-array duality, C-strings, and standard string library functions.ArraysC-stringsstring.hPointer arithmetic
    04Pointers & MemoryThe heart of C. Pointer types, dereferencing, pointer-to-pointer, and visualising memory layouts.* and &Pointer typesvoid*NULL
    05Dynamic MemoryHeap allocation with malloc/calloc/realloc, freeing memory properly, and detecting memory leaks with Valgrind.mallocfreeValgrindMemory leaks
    06Structs & UnionsCustom data types, struct padding, typedef, and building simple linked data structures from scratch.structuniontypedefPadding
    07File I/Ofopen, fread, fwrite, fprintf — reading and writing binary and text files, and error handling with errno.fopen/fcloseBinary fileserrnofseek
    08Data Structures in CImplement linked lists, stacks, queues, and a basic hash table using only C — no libraries, no shortcuts.Linked ListStackQueueHash Table
  



  
    Memory Model
    You'll actually understand this.
    Most courses skip over memory. This one makes it the centrepiece — because everything else flows from it.
    
      Stack — grows downward
      0x7fff...f0main()← current frame
      0x7fff...d0int x = 42local variable
      0x7fff...c8int *p← pointer on stack
      Heap — grows upward
      0x55...1000malloc(100)← *p points here
      0x55...1064[free block]must call free()
    
  



  Projects
  Four Things You'll Build in C
  No toy programs. Each project solves a real problem and teaches you something you can't learn from reading.
  
    Project 01Custom Shell (mini-bash)Build a minimal Unix shell that reads commands, forks processes, handles pipes and redirects. Real systems programming.fork/execpipessignals
    Project 02Memory AllocatorImplement your own malloc() and free() using sbrk. Handle fragmentation, coalescing, and alignment correctly.sbrkHeapFragmentation
    Project 03Linked List LibraryA complete doubly-linked list with insert, delete, search, and iterator — with tests for every function.StructsPointersTesting
    Project 04File Compression ToolImplement run-length encoding to compress and decompress binary files. Process I/O and bitwise operations.File I/OBitwise opsRLE
  



  Track 01 · Enrolling
  Understand code at thedeepest level.
  C makes every other language feel transparent. Once you know C, you know how computing actually works.

        The order of topics is deliberate. C before C++. Recursion before DP. Each session builds on the last — no random jumps that leave gaps in your understanding.
      
      
        04

        Everything you build is yours. A live website, a GitHub full of real projects, a Quarto portfolio. By the end, you can show your work — not just talk about it.

  Common Track · Foundation
  
    Git &
    GitHub
    for everyone.
  
  Version control is not optional. Git is the language every developer speaks — and GitHub is where your work lives, gets reviewed, and gets hired. Start here, regardless of what you plan to build.
  
    ⏱ Duration 2 Weeks
    👥 Level All Levels
    📦 Projects 3 Real Repos

    View Curriculum
  




  
    Why This First
    Version control is thefirst professional skill.
    Every other course involves code. That code needs to be tracked, backed up, collaborated on, and shipped. Git is how that happens. Employers expect it from day one.
    
      01Never Lose Work AgainGit creates a complete history of every change. Roll back mistakes, compare versions, and work fearlessly knowing you can always recover.
      02Collaborate Like a ProBranching and merging let multiple people work on the same project simultaneously — without overwriting each other. This is how every real team works.
      03GitHub is Your PortfolioEvery project you build in every other course goes on GitHub. Recruiters look at your commit history. A green contribution graph speaks louder than a resume.
      04CI/CD and AutomationGitHub Actions lets your code test, build, and deploy itself. You'll use it in the AWS and Web Development courses — but it starts here.
    
  




  Core Commands
  What You'll Master
  You won't just memorise commands — you'll understand why each one exists and build muscle memory through real projects.
  
    
      Setup & Init
      Repository Basics
      Create repos, configure identity, understand the working tree, staging area, and commit history.
      git init · git clone · git config
    
    
      Daily Workflow
      Add, Commit, Push
      Stage changes selectively, write meaningful commit messages, and push to remote repositories.
      git add · git commit · git push
    
    
      Branching
      Branches & Merging
      Feature branches, merge strategies (fast-forward vs three-way), resolving conflicts, and rebase basics.
      git branch · git merge · git rebase
    
    
      Collaboration
      Pull Requests & Reviews
      Fork workflows, opening PRs, reviewing code, requesting changes, and squash merges.
      gh pr create · git fetch · git pull
    
    
      History & Debugging
      Log, Diff & Blame
      Navigate commit history, compare file versions, find who changed what and when using git blame and bisect.
      git log · git diff · git blame
    
    
      Advanced
      Stash, Tags & GitHub Actions
      Temporarily stash work, tag releases, and write a basic CI pipeline that runs tests on every push.
      git stash · git tag · gh workflow
    
  




  
    2-Week Schedule
    Session by Session
    Compact, intensive, and immediately applicable. By the end of week two, every other course will feel natural because you already know how to manage your code.
    
      W1Week 1 · Core GitLocal Git Masteryinit, add, commit, diff, log, stash, branching, and merging on your local machineSessions 1–3
      W2Week 2 · GitHubRemote Repos, PRs & GitHub ActionsPush to GitHub, fork, open a PR, review code, and write your first CI/CD workflowSessions 4–6
      ✦CapstoneCollaborative Project SubmissionWork in pairs on a shared repo — branch, PR, review and merge. Your first open-source style contribution.End of Week 2

  Your code deservesa proper home.
  Git & GitHub is the foundation every course builds on. Enroll in this track first — it takes two weeks and pays off for the rest of your career.
